Here is Solomon’s design to build a temple. There is no adversary, no Satan, so it is said; no instrument of Satan to oppose it or divert from it. Satan does all he can to hinder temple work. When there is no evil abroad, then let us be ready and active in that which is good, and make progress. Let God’s promises quicken our efforts. And all outward skill and advantages should be made useful for the interests of Christ's kingdom. If Tyre supplies Israel with craftsmen, Israel will supply Tyre with corn. Thus, by the wise disposal of Providence, one country has need of another and is benefitted by another, so that there may be dependence on one another to the glory of God.

The temple was chiefly built by the riches and labor of Gentiles, which typified their being called into the church. Solomon commanded, and they brought costly stones for the foundation. Christ, who is laid for a Foundation, is a chosen and precious Stone. We should lay our foundation firm and bestow most pains on that part of our religion which lies out of the sight of men. And happy those who, as lively stones, are built up into a spiritual house, for a habitation of God through the Spirit. Who among us will build in the house of the Lord?
